methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $430M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $2.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B+

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B+

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Fervo Energy is a Series C climate & energy company founded by Tim Latimer and Jack Norbeck that has raised $430M+ to date. The valuation assessment references a peer universe of nine comparable companies: one public (LNZA) and eight private firms, including same-stage comparables KoBold Metals ($2.0B), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B), and Amogy ($700M).

Two valuation methods are applicable and triangulated. The stage-progression method applies a 1.7x multiplier to the $430M raised proxy, implying a valuation of $731M. The peer-median approach uses three same-sector, same-stage peers with a median valuation of $1.0B (range: $700M–$2.0B), directly implying $1.0B. These methods produce a triangulated range with a low of $621M, median of $866M, and high of $1.1B.

The median implied valuation of $866M reflects the gap between stage-progression and peer benchmarks. Key uncertainties include the precise composition and maturity profile of the three peer comparables—KoBold Metals' $2.0B valuation materially influences the range—and whether Fervo's capital intensity and technology risk align with the peer set's risk profile. The $245M spread between low and high estimates ($621M–$1.1B) underscores sensitivity to peer selection and stage-specific growth assumptions.
